question: This law protects women from domestic violence in India.

metadata.source: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.document_name: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md

question: The law was passed in the year 2000.

metadata.source: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.document_name: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md

question: The law protects women from violence only outside the family.

metadata.source: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.document_name: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md

question: The law starts working immediately after it is passed by Parliament.

metadata.source: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.document_name: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md

question: The law can only be enforced after the President signs it.

metadata.source: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.document_name: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md

question: An aggrieved person under this law must be currently in a domestic relationship with the respondent.

metadata.source: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.document_name: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md

question: A child includes any person below the age of eighteen years, whether natural, adopted, step, or foster.

metadata.source: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.document_name: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md

question: A respondent can only be an adult male person who has been in a domestic relationship with the aggrieved person.

metadata.source: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.document_name: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md

question: A shared household includes a home owned or rented by the respondent, even if the aggrieved person has no legal claim to it.

metadata.source: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.document_name: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md

question: Physical abuse includes causing pain or harm to the body.

metadata.source: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.document_name: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.chapter_title: POWERS AND DUTIES OF PROTECTION OFFICERS, SERVICE PROVIDERS, ETC.

question: Economic abuse only involves withholding money from the aggrieved person.

metadata.source: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.document_name: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.chapter_title: POWERS AND DUTIES OF PROTECTION OFFICERS, SERVICE PROVIDERS, ETC.

question: Verbal and emotional abuse includes insults about not having a male child.

metadata.source: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.document_name: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.chapter_title: POWERS AND DUTIES OF PROTECTION OFFICERS, SERVICE PROVIDERS, ETC.

question: Domestic violence can only occur if the harasser lives with the victim.

metadata.source: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.document_name: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.chapter_title: POWERS AND DUTIES OF PROTECTION OFFICERS, SERVICE PROVIDERS, ETC.

question: A person can be held legally responsible for giving false information to a Protection Officer in good faith.

metadata.source: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.document_name: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.chapter_title: POWERS AND DUTIES OF PROTECTION OFFICERS, SERVICE PROVIDERS, ETC.
metadata.section_title: Information to Protection Officer and exclusion of liability of informant .

question: If a person lies to a Protection Officer while thinking they are telling the truth, they can still be punished.

metadata.source: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.document_name: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.chapter_title: POWERS AND DUTIES OF PROTECTION OFFICERS, SERVICE PROVIDERS, ETC.
metadata.section_title: Information to Protection Officer and exclusion of liability of informant .

question: The law protects informants from being sued or prosecuted for giving information to a Protection Officer in good faith.

metadata.source: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.document_name: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.chapter_title: POWERS AND DUTIES OF PROTECTION OFFICERS, SERVICE PROVIDERS, ETC.
metadata.section_title: Information to Protection Officer and exclusion of liability of informant .

question: Police officers must inform victims about their right to apply for a protection order under the Act.

metadata.source: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.document_name: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.chapter_title: POWERS AND DUTIES OF PROTECTION OFFICERS, SERVICE PROVIDERS, ETC.
metadata.section_title: Duties of police officers, service providers and Magistrate .

question: The Act allows police officers to ignore their legal duty to investigate crimes even if they receive information about a cognizable offence.

metadata.source: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.document_name: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.chapter_title: POWERS AND DUTIES OF PROTECTION OFFICERS, SERVICE PROVIDERS, ETC.
metadata.section_title: Duties of police officers, service providers and Magistrate .

question: Victims must be told about the availability of free legal services under the Legal Services Authorities Act, 1987.

metadata.source: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.document_name: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.chapter_title: POWERS AND DUTIES OF PROTECTION OFFICERS, SERVICE PROVIDERS, ETC.
metadata.section_title: Duties of police officers, service providers and Magistrate .

question: The Act requires police officers to inform victims about services of service providers but does not require informing about Protection Officers.

metadata.source: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.document_name: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.chapter_title: POWERS AND DUTIES OF PROTECTION OFFICERS, SERVICE PROVIDERS, ETC.
metadata.section_title: Duties of police officers, service providers and Magistrate .

question: Protection Officers must be women.

metadata.source: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.document_name: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.chapter_title: POWERS AND DUTIES OF PROTECTION OFFICERS, SERVICE PROVIDERS, ETC.

question: The qualifications and experience for Protection Officers are not specified in the context.

metadata.source: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.document_name: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.chapter_title: POWERS AND DUTIES OF PROTECTION OFFICERS, SERVICE PROVIDERS, ETC.

question: The terms and conditions of service for Protection Officers are fixed and cannot be changed.

metadata.source: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.document_name: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.chapter_title: POWERS AND DUTIES OF PROTECTION OFFICERS, SERVICE PROVIDERS, ETC.

question: A Protection Officer must assist the Magistrate in performing their functions under the Domestic Violence Act.

metadata.source: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.document_name: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.chapter_title: POWERS AND DUTIES OF PROTECTION OFFICERS, SERVICE PROVIDERS, ETC.
metadata.section_title: Duties and functions of Protection Officers .

question: A Protection Officer can make a domestic incident report only if the police officer approves it first.

metadata.source: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.document_name: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.chapter_title: POWERS AND DUTIES OF PROTECTION OFFICERS, SERVICE PROVIDERS, ETC.
metadata.section_title: Duties and functions of Protection Officers .

question: A Protection Officer must ensure the aggrieved person receives free legal aid if they request it.

metadata.source: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.document_name: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.chapter_title: POWERS AND DUTIES OF PROTECTION OFFICERS, SERVICE PROVIDERS, ETC.
metadata.section_title: Duties and functions of Protection Officers .

question: A Protection Officer is required to personally medically examine the aggrieved person if they have injuries.

metadata.source: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.document_name: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.chapter_title: POWERS AND DUTIES OF PROTECTION OFFICERS, SERVICE PROVIDERS, ETC.
metadata.section_title: Duties and functions of Protection Officers .

question: A service provider can record a domestic incident report only if the victim requests it.

metadata.source: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.document_name: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.chapter_title: POWERS AND DUTIES OF PROTECTION OFFICERS, SERVICE PROVIDERS, ETC.

question: A service provider must ensure the victim is provided shelter in a shelter home if she wants it.

metadata.source: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.document_name: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.chapter_title: POWERS AND DUTIES OF PROTECTION OFFICERS, SERVICE PROVIDERS, ETC.

question: A service provider can forward a medical report to the police station and Protection Officer if the victim is medically examined.

metadata.source: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.document_name: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.chapter_title: POWERS AND DUTIES OF PROTECTION OFFICERS, SERVICE PROVIDERS, ETC.

question: A service provider can be sued for actions taken in good faith under this Act.

metadata.source: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.document_name: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.chapter_title: POWERS AND DUTIES OF PROTECTION OFFICERS, SERVICE PROVIDERS, ETC.

question: The government must publicize the provisions of this Act through television, radio, and print media regularly.

metadata.source: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.document_name: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md

question: Police officers are not required to participate in sensitization and awareness training on the issues addressed by this Act.

metadata.source: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.document_name: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md

question: Effective coordination between Ministries and Departments must be established to address issues of domestic violence.

metadata.source: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.document_name: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md

question: Protocols for Ministries delivering services to women under this Act do not need to be prepared or put in place.

metadata.source: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.document_name: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md

question: The Magistrate must consider a domestic incident report before making any order on an application under this law.

metadata.source: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.document_name: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md

question: A Magistrate can order compensation or damages even if the applicant has not filed a separate lawsuit for the same injury.

metadata.source: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.document_name: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md

question: If a court has already awarded compensation, the Magistrate's order for compensation cannot be enforced at all.

metadata.source: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.document_name: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md

question: Every application under this law must follow a strict government-prescribed form.

metadata.source: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.document_name: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md

question: A Protection Officer’s declaration of service is automatically proof that notice was served.

metadata.source: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.document_name: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md

question: The Magistrate can direct that notice also be served on other persons.

metadata.source: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.document_name: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md

question: The Protection Officer’s declaration of service is final and cannot be challenged.

metadata.source: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.document_name: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md

question: The form of the declaration of service must follow rules set by the Magistrate.

metadata.source: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.document_name: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md

question: The Magistrate must set the next court date within two months after giving a direction under Section 14.

metadata.source: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.document_name: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md

question: The Magistrate can delay setting the next hearing date beyond two months if the case is complex.

metadata.source: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.document_name: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md

question: If the Magistrate issues a direction under Section 14, the next hearing date must be fixed immediately.

metadata.source: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.document_name: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md

question: The time limit for fixing the next hearing date is strictly not more than two months.

metadata.source: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.document_name: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md

question: The respondent can evict the aggrieved person from the shared household without following any legal procedure.

metadata.source: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.document_name: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.section_title: Right to reside in a shared household.

question: The shared household includes only the parts of the home where the respondent lives.

metadata.source: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.document_name: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.section_title: Right to reside in a shared household.

question: The respondent must follow a legal procedure to evict or exclude the aggrieved person from the shared household.

metadata.source: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.document_name: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.section_title: Right to reside in a shared household.

question: A protection order can stop someone from entering the workplace of the person they are accused of abusing.

metadata.source: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.document_name: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md

question: The law allows the respondent to take money from a shared bank account without permission if the aggrieved person does not object.

metadata.source: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.document_name: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md

question: A person can be protected from all forms of communication, including emails or phone calls, under a protection order.

metadata.source: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.document_name: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md

question: The protection order only applies if the abuser lives in the same home as the victim.

metadata.source: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.document_name: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md

question: A restraining order can prevent a respondent from removing the aggrieved person from the shared household, even if the respondent owns the house.

metadata.source: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.document_name: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md

question: The Magistrate cannot pass an order directing the respondent to leave the shared household if the respondent is a woman.

metadata.source: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.document_name: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md

question: A residence order can require the respondent to pay rent for alternate accommodation for the aggrieved person, but only if the respondent agrees.

metadata.source: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.document_name: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md

question: The Magistrate can impose additional conditions to protect the aggrieved person, even if those conditions are not specifically listed in the law.

metadata.source: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.document_name: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md

question: Monetary relief can include the loss of earnings suffered by the aggrieved person.

metadata.source: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.document_name: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md

question: The Magistrate can order a lump sum payment or monthly payments of maintenance.

metadata.source: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.document_name: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md

question: The Magistrate must send a copy of the monetary relief order only to the aggrieved person.

metadata.source: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.document_name: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md

question: If the respondent fails to pay the monetary relief, the Magistrate can direct the employer to pay the aggrieved person directly from the respondent's wages.

metadata.source: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.document_name: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md

question: The Magistrate must allow visits if the respondent requests them.

metadata.source: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.document_name: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md

question: A Magistrate can refuse a visit if they believe it may harm the child.

metadata.source: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.document_name: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md

question: The Magistrate can allow visits even if the child's interests are at risk.

metadata.source: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.document_name: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md

question: The Magistrate can grant an ex parte order only if the respondent has already committed an act of domestic violence.

metadata.source: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.document_name: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.section_title: Power to grant interim and _ex parte_ orders .

question: An ex parte order can be granted based on the affidavit of the aggrieved person without the respondent being present.

metadata.source: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.document_name: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.section_title: Power to grant interim and _ex parte_ orders .

question: The Magistrate must be satisfied that there is a likelihood the respondent may commit an act of domestic violence before granting an ex parte order.

metadata.source: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.document_name: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.section_title: Power to grant interim and _ex parte_ orders .

question: An ex parte order can be granted under Section 23 even if the affidavit does not disclose any act of domestic violence.

metadata.source: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.document_name: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.section_title: Power to grant interim and _ex parte_ orders .

question: The court must provide copies of orders free of cost.

metadata.source: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.document_name: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.section_title: Court to give copies of order free of cost.

question: Courts can charge a small fee for providing copies of orders.

metadata.source: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.document_name: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.section_title: Court to give copies of order free of cost.

question: Section 24 requires courts to give copies of orders without any condition.

metadata.source: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.document_name: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.section_title: Court to give copies of order free of cost.

question: The rule applies to all types of court orders.

metadata.source: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.document_name: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.section_title: Court to give copies of order free of cost.

question: The Magistrate can change an order if both the aggrieved person and the respondent agree.

metadata.source: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.document_name: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md

question: The Magistrate must record reasons in writing before altering an order.

metadata.source: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.document_name: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md

question: The Magistrate can alter an order only if there is a change in circumstances.

metadata.source: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.document_name: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md

question: The Magistrate can revoke an order even if no one applies for it.

metadata.source: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.document_name: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md

question: A person can ask for relief under Section 26 in the same case where they are also seeking other types of relief.

metadata.source: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.document_name: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.section_title: Relief in other suits and legal proceedings .

question: If a person gets relief from a court that is not under this Act, they do not need to tell the Magistrate about it.

metadata.source: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.document_name: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.section_title: Relief in other suits and legal proceedings .

question: Relief under Section 26 can only be sought in criminal court proceedings.

metadata.source: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.document_name: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.section_title: Relief in other suits and legal proceedings .

question: The Magistrate must be informed if relief was granted in another legal proceeding.

metadata.source: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.document_name: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.section_title: Relief in other suits and legal proceedings .

question: A protection order under this Act can only be granted by a court where the respondent lives.

metadata.source: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.document_name: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md

question: If the cause of action happened in Delhi, a court in Delhi can grant a protection order under this Act.

metadata.source: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.document_name: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md

question: An order made under this Act is valid only in the state where it was issued.

metadata.source: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.document_name: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md

question: A court can grant a protection order if the person seeking it works in that court's area, even if they do not live there.

metadata.source: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.document_name: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md

question: The court has the power to create its own procedure for handling applications under Section 12.

metadata.source: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.document_name: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md

question: The court must follow the procedure set in sub-section (1) and cannot change it for any application.

metadata.source: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.document_name: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md

question: The court can set its own procedure for disposal of applications under sub-section (2) of Section 23.

metadata.source: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.document_name: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md

question: Sub-section (1) completely prevents the court from making any changes to the standard procedure for disposal of applications.

metadata.source: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.document_name: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md

question: An appeal is a request to a higher court to review a lower court's decision.

metadata.source: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.document_name: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md

question: Section 29 allows any person to appeal any court decision without restrictions.

metadata.source: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.document_name: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md

question: Appeals can only be made if the original court agrees to the request.

metadata.source: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.document_name: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md

question: The purpose of an appeal is to correct errors made in the original trial.

metadata.source: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.document_name: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md

question: Protection officers under Section 30 are considered public servants.

metadata.source: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.document_name: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.section_title: Protection Officers and members of service providers to be public servants .

question: Members of service providers are not covered under Section 30.

metadata.source: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.document_name: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.section_title: Protection Officers and members of service providers to be public servants .

question: Section 30 applies only to protection officers and not to their assistants.

metadata.source: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.document_name: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.section_title: Protection Officers and members of service providers to be public servants .

question: Protection officers and service provider members have legal immunity like other public servants.

metadata.source: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.document_name: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.section_title: Protection Officers and members of service providers to be public servants .

question: The Magistrate who originally passed the protection order must try the breach case if possible.

metadata.source: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.document_name: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.section_title: Penalty for breach of protection order by respondent.

question: The Magistrate can only try charges for breaching the protection order under this section.

metadata.source: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.document_name: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.section_title: Penalty for breach of protection order by respondent.

question: The Magistrate may also charge the accused under the Dowry Prohibition Act if the facts support it.

metadata.source: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.document_name: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.section_title: Penalty for breach of protection order by respondent.

question: The court can use only the victim's statement to decide if a crime under section 31(1) was committed.

metadata.source: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.document_name: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md

question: The court must have additional evidence besides the victim's testimony to prove the crime.

metadata.source: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.document_name: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md

question: Section 32 allows the court to consider the victim's statement alone for proving the offence.

metadata.source: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.document_name: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md

question: The victim's testimony alone is not sufficient for the court to find the accused guilty.

metadata.source: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.document_name: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md

question: A Protection Officer can be penalized for failing to perform their duty.

metadata.source: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.document_name: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.section_title: Penalty for not discharging duty by Protection Officer.

question: Penalty under Section 33 applies only if the Protection Officer intentionally ignores their duty.

metadata.source: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.document_name: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.section_title: Penalty for not discharging duty by Protection Officer.

question: A Protection Officer cannot be penalized if the duty is not discharged due to lack of resources.

metadata.source: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.document_name: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.section_title: Penalty for not discharging duty by Protection Officer.

question: A Protection Officer can be prosecuted for an offence committed during duty without prior sanction.

metadata.source: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.document_name: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.section_title: Cognizance of offence committed by Protection Officer .

question: No court can take cognizance of an offence committed by a Protection Officer unless a complaint is made by the designated authority.

metadata.source: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.document_name: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.section_title: Cognizance of offence committed by Protection Officer .

question: A complaint against a Protection Officer must be filed within 6 months of the offence being committed.

metadata.source: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.document_name: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.section_title: Cognizance of offence committed by Protection Officer .

question: The designated authority has the power to grant sanction for prosecuting a Protection Officer.

metadata.source: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.document_name: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.section_title: Cognizance of offence committed by Protection Officer .

question: Protection under Section 35 applies only if the action is taken with a good intention.

metadata.source: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.document_name: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.section_title: Protection of action taken in good faith .

question: Section 35 protects all actions taken by a person, even if done maliciously.

metadata.source: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.document_name: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.section_title: Protection of action taken in good faith .

question: Good faith under Section 35 means the person acted honestly, regardless of the outcome.

metadata.source: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.document_name: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.section_title: Protection of action taken in good faith .

question: Protection under Section 35 is automatic and does not require any conditions to be met.

metadata.source: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.document_name: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.section_title: Protection of action taken in good faith .

question: This law replaces all other existing laws.

metadata.source: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.document_name: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.section_title: Act not in derogation of any other law.

question: This law does not cancel out other laws unless it says so.

metadata.source: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.document_name: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.section_title: Act not in derogation of any other law.

question: If another law conflicts with this one, this law will automatically override it.

metadata.source: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.document_name: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.section_title: Act not in derogation of any other law.

question: This law is meant to work together with other laws, not against them.

metadata.source: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.document_name: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.section_title: Act not in derogation of any other law.

question: The Central Government can make rules about the qualifications required for a Protection Officer.

metadata.source: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.document_name: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.section_title: Power of Central Government to make rules.

question: Rules made under this Act do not need to be approved by Parliament.

metadata.source: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.document_name: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.section_title: Power of Central Government to make rules.

question: The form for filing an application for a protection order is specified in the rules under this section.

metadata.source: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.document_name: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.section_title: Power of Central Government to make rules.

question: Modifications made by Parliament to a rule do not affect actions already taken under that rule.

metadata.source: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.document_name: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.md
metadata.section_title: Power of Central Government to make rules.
